what: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: Generates comprehensive descriptive statistics table given dataframe, optionally stratified categorical variable. Provides customisable summaries continuous categorical variables, including control rounding, missing data reporting, labelling, formatting, table aesthetics. output publication-ready gtsummary / gt table. add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE, p-values computed outside gtsummary using run_diff (continuous variables) run_assoc (categorical variables), injected table. p-value cell carries letter superscript identifying exact test used; legend appended table footnotes. Variables p-value computed shown ---† corresponding footnote.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
code: run_summarytable(   x,   summarize_what = NULL,   split_by = NULL,   filter = NULL,   split_by_header = NULL,   strata_by = NULL,   rename_variables = NULL,   combine_categories = NULL,   continuous_statistics = "meanSD",   categorical_statistics = "n_percent",   force_continuous = NULL,   force_categorical = NULL,   n_digits_continuous = c(2, 2),   n_digits_categorical = c(0, 2),   zero_as_exp = TRUE,   display_missing = "ifany",   missing_text = "No data/missing",   missing_stat = "n_percent",   include_missing_in_splits = FALSE,   sort_categorical_variables_by = "alphanumeric",   calc_percent_by = "column",   calc_col_percent_using = "n_valid_in_column",   add_inferential_pvalues = FALSE,   test_type_continuous = c("auto", "parametric", "nonparametric"),   test_type_categorical = c("auto", "chisq", "fisher"),   paired = FALSE,   n_digits_pvalues = 3,   bold_significant_pvalues = TRUE,   bold_significant_pvalues_at = 0.05,   header = "Variable",   show_n_header = TRUE,   bold_labels = TRUE,   italicize_levels = TRUE,   clean_table = TRUE,   table_name = "auto" )

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: x Dataframe. input dataframe. summarize_what Character vector. Column names include analysis. NULL (default), columns except named split_by strata_by included. split_by String. column name x (must categorical least 2 unique levels) stratify table. Required add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. Default NULL. filter Optional character vector levels split_by exclude analysis. x run_DIpreprocess object, defaults identified QC types. split_by_header String. Display header split_by variable. Defaults value split_by. strata_by String. column name x (must categorical least 2 unique levels) stratify split_by. combined add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. Default NULL. rename_variables List. Formulas form list("original" ~ "new", ...) used relabel variables table. combine_categories Named list named lists. Used combine levels categorical variables. top-level names correspond column names x. column's value named list names new category labels values character vectors original levels combine. column categorical default, must listed force_categorical first. Defaults NULL. Example format:  combine_categories = list(   Education = list(     "Higher Ed" = c("Bachelors", "Masters"),     "Schooling" = c("Elementary", "High School")   ),   Gender = list(     "Non-Male" = c("Female", "")   ) ) continuous_statistics String. Summary statistic(s) continuous variables. One : "meanSD", "meanSD2", "medianIQR", "mean", "sd", "median", "p0", "p25", "p50", "p75", "p100", "IQR". Default "meanSD". categorical_statistics String. Summary statistic(s) categorical variables. One : "n_percent", "n", "percent". Default "n_percent". force_continuous Character vector. Column names treat continuous regardless storage type. coercion applied passing data run_diff. force_categorical Character vector. Column names treat categorical regardless storage type. coercion applied passing data run_assoc. n_digits_continuous Numeric vector c(d1, d2) "dynamic". Number decimal places first subsequent numeric tokens continuous summary cells (e.g., mean SD). Default c(2, 2). n_digits_categorical Numeric vector c(d1, d2) "dynamic". Decimal places count percentage categorical summary cells. Default c(0, 2). zero_as_exp Logical. TRUE (default), continuous summary tokens round 0 requested precision genuinely non-zero displayed scientific notation (e.g., 1.00e-3). display_missing String. report missing values: "ifany" (default), "", "always". missing_text String. Label missing-value rows. Default "data/missing". missing_stat String. Format missing-value counts: one "n_percent" (default), "n", "percent". include_missing_in_splits Logical. TRUE, missing values split_by / strata_by columns kept explicit level. Defaults FALSE. sort_categorical_variables_by String. Level ordering categorical variables: "alphanumeric" (default) "frequency". calc_percent_by String. Percentage base categorical variables: "column" (default), "row", "cell", "total". "total" calculates percentages based total number samples, study participants, simply number rows x. calc_col_percent_using String. Denominator calc_percent_by = "column": "n_valid_in_column" (default) "n_in_column". add_inferential_pvalues Logical. TRUE, p-values computed via run_diff (continuous variables) run_assoc (categorical variables) injected table. Requires split_by non-NULL strata_by NULL. Default FALSE. test_type_continuous String. Test strategy forwarded run_diff add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. One "auto" (default), "parametric", "nonparametric". See run_diff details. test_type_categorical String. Test strategy forwarded run_assoc add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE. One "auto" (default), "chisq", "fisher". See run_assoc details. paired Logical. TRUE, paired tests used computing p-values. Forwarded run_diff run_assoc. Note descriptive statistics shown table body always unpaired summaries. Default FALSE. n_digits_pvalues Numeric NULL. Decimal places displayed p-values. Default 3. Set NULL automatic formatting (3 d.p. \(p < 0.10\), otherwise 1 d.p.). bold_significant_pvalues Logical. TRUE (default), p-values bold_significant_pvalues_at bolded. bold_significant_pvalues_at Numeric. Significance threshold bolding. Default 0.05. header String. Header variable-label column. Default "Variable". show_n_header Logical. TRUE (default), appends total sample size header (e.g., "Variable (N = 100)"). bold_labels Logical. TRUE (default), variable labels bolded. italicize_levels Logical. TRUE (default), categorical level labels italicised. clean_table Logical. TRUE (default), cells containing zero count (e.g., 0 (0.00%)) replaced empty strings. table_name String NULL. Table title. "auto" (default) generates title automatically. NULL suppresses title.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: gt_tbl object (via gtsummary::as_gt()) representing descriptive (optionally inferential) statistics table.

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Performs Descriptive Statistics on a Dataframe — run_summarytable
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(gtsummary) library(dplyr)  set.seed(123) sample_df <- data.frame(   Age       = sample(c(18:65, NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(rep(1, 48), 5)),   Gender    = sample(c("Male", "Female", NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.48, 0.48, 0.04)),   Education = sample(c("Elementary", "High School",                         "Bachelors", "Masters", NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.40, 0.30, 0.15, 0.10, 0.05)),   Income    = sample(c(seq(5000, 40000, by = 5000), NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE, prob = c(rep(1, 8), 2)),   Smoker    = sample(c(1, 0, NA), 100, replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.25, 0.65, 0.10)),   Region    = sample(c("North", "South", "East", "West", NA), 100,                      replace = TRUE,                      prob = c(0.24, 0.24, 0.24, 0.24, .04)) )  # Basic descriptive statistics (no grouping) sample_df |> run_summarytable()  # Descriptive statistics split by Gender sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by        = "Gender",     split_by_header = "Participant Gender",     rename_variables = list(Age    ~ "Age (Years)",                             Income ~ "Annual Income",                             Smoker ~ "Smoking Status")   )  # Median and IQR for continuous variables sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     continuous_statistics  = "medianIQR",     categorical_statistics = "n",     n_digits_continuous    = c(0, 0),     n_digits_categorical   = c(0, 0)   )  # Inferential p-values using run_diff (continuous) and run_assoc # (categorical), auto test selection sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE   )  # Force non-parametric tests for continuous variables sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE,     test_type_continuous    = "nonparametric",     test_type_categorical   = "fisher"   )  # Paired inferential p-values (descriptive body remains unpaired) sample_df |>   run_summarytable(     split_by                = "Gender",     add_inferential_pvalues = TRUE,     paired                  = TRUE   ) } # }

what: Convert a Named Vector to a Two-Column Data Frame — run_vector2df
title: Convert a Named Vector to a Two-Column Data Frame — run_vector2df
text: Transforms named vector tidy two-column data frame, one column holds names holds corresponding values. Optionally filters rows value threshold range. Useful quickly tabulating named outputs VIP scores, loadings, named numeric/character vector.

title: Convert a Named Vector to a Two-Column Data Frame — run_vector2df
code: run_vector2df(   x,   name = "Variable",   value = "Value",   sort_by = c("none", "name", "value_asc", "value_desc"),   min = NULL,   max = NULL,   range_type = c("inner", "outer"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )

title: Convert a Named Vector to a Two-Column Data Frame — run_vector2df
text: x named vector (numeric, integer, character, logical). Must names; error raised names(x) NULL. name single character string giving name column contain vector names. Defaults "Variable". value single character string giving name column contain vector values. Defaults "Value". sort_by single character string controlling row ordering. One "none" (original order), "name" (alphabetical name column), "value_asc" (ascending value), "value_desc" (descending value). Defaults "none". min single numeric value used lower threshold filtering value column. Behaviour depends range_type: "inner": rows value >= min kept (max NULL), rows satisfying value >= min & value <= max (supplied). "outer": rows value <= min kept (max NULL), rows satisfying value <= min | value >= max (supplied). Defaults NULL (lower filtering). max single numeric value used upper threshold filtering value column. Behaviour depends range_type: "inner": rows value <= max kept (min NULL), rows satisfying value >= min & value <= max (supplied). "outer": rows value >= max kept (min NULL), rows satisfying value <= min | value >= max (supplied). Defaults NULL (upper filtering). range_type single character string, either "inner" "outer", controls min max applied supplied. "inner" keeps values thresholds (inclusive); "outer" keeps values outside thresholds (inclusive). Ignored one min/max supplied. Defaults "inner". stringsAsFactors Logical. TRUE, name column returned factor. Defaults FALSE.

title: Convert a Named Vector to a Two-Column Data Frame — run_vector2df
text: data.frame two columns named according name value, one row per (retained) element x. Row names reset sequential integers.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Exports one data frames, tibbles, matrices single .xlsx file (.csv single-object exports).  x named list, tabular element written worksheet.  pondeR result objects (classes beginning run_) automatically unpacked extract tabular components. x contains direct tables holds list tables one level deep, nested list transparently unwrapped.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
code: save_excel(   x,   filename = "output",   folder = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   freeze_first_row = TRUE,   freeze_first_col = FALSE,   auto_col_width = TRUE,   fd_date_stamp = FALSE,   fd_time_stamp = FALSE,   fn_date_stamp = FALSE,   fn_time_stamp = FALSE )

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: x data frame, tibble, matrix, pondeR result object (e.g. output run_auc(), run_diff()), named list whose elements combination types.  One level nesting automatically unpacked direct tabular elements found top level. filename single character string output file name.  Used -x single object.  Ignored x list multiple elements — element's name used sheet name list object's name becomes file name.  Illegal characters removed silently.  Defaults "output" supplied. folder single character string giving target directory. Supports nested folders (e.g., "Main folder/sub folder"). Created recursively absent. Defaults NULL (current working directory). overwrite Logical.  TRUE existing file silently overwritten.  FALSE (default) Windows-style counter suffix appended, e.g. "results (1).xlsx". freeze_first_row Logical.  TRUE (default) first row every worksheet frozen. freeze_first_col Logical.  TRUE first column every worksheet frozen.  Defaults FALSE. auto_col_width Logical.  TRUE (default) column widths auto-fitted. fd_date_stamp Logical.  Appends "MmmDDYYYY" date folder.  Default FALSE. fd_time_stamp Logical.  Appends "HHMM" time folder.  Default FALSE. fn_date_stamp Logical.  Appends date file name.  Default FALSE. fn_time_stamp Logical.  Appends time file name.  Default FALSE.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Called side effect.  Returns full normalised file path invisibly character string.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: Sheet naming.  Sheet names taken names(x). Illegal Excel characters stripped, names truncated 25 characters (vowels removed end first, consonants needed), reserved name "History" becomes "History_", duplicates get " (N)" suffix (" (999)", hence 25-character base limit). Filename priority.  filename argument prioritized unless left default "output".  filename "output" x list (pondeR result containing multiple tables), function automatically uses variable name x filename convenience. pondeR result objects.  Objects whose class starts run_ (e.g. run_auc, run_diff) unpacked automatically tabular slots written separate sheets.

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Save Data Frames, Tibbles, or Matrices to an Excel File — save_excel
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single data frame — filename is respected save_excel(mtcars, filename = "mtcars_export")  # Named list — filename is ignored; sheet names come from list names sheets <- list(Cars = mtcars, Iris = iris) save_excel(sheets, filename = "ignored")  # pondeR result object res <- run_auc(data = mydata, response = "group", predictors = c("A","B")) save_excel(res, filename = "auc_results")  # With date + time stamp on the file name save_excel(iris, filename = "iris", fn_date_stamp = TRUE, fn_time_stamp = TRUE) } # }

what: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Exports single plot named list plots individual files. Supported plot types include ggplot2 objects, plotly widgets, grid grobs/gtables, recordedplot objects, zero-argument base-R drawing functions, pondeR result objects produced functions plot_diff(), run_auc(), plot_score(), plot_scree(), plot_volcano(). x single plot, filename used output file name.  x list plots, filename ignored element's list name used instead.  x contains direct plot objects top level holds list plots one level deep (e.g. result$plots), nested list transparently unpacked.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
code: save_plots(   x,   filename = "plot",   format = NULL,   width = 15,   height = 12,   dpi = 300,   folder = NULL,   transparent = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   fd_date_stamp = FALSE,   fd_time_stamp = FALSE,   fn_date_stamp = FALSE,   fn_time_stamp = FALSE )

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: x single plot object named list plot objects.  Accepted types: ggplot, plotly, grob/gtable, recordedplot, zero-argument base-R drawing function, pondeR result object.  One level list nesting automatically unpacked direct plot objects found top level. filename single character string output file name.  Used x single plot.  x multi-plot list argument ignored element's name used file name. extension filename sets format unless format also supplied.  Defaults "plot" omitted. format Optional single character string overriding file extension exported files, e.g. "pdf", "svg", "png".  Case-insensitive; accepts without leading dot. supplied, extension filename ignored. width Numeric.  Output width inches.  Default 15. height Numeric.  Output height inches.  Default 12. dpi Numeric.  Resolution dots per inch raster formats. Default 300.  Ignored vector formats. folder single character string target directory. Supports nested folders (e.g., "Main folder/sub folder"). Created recursively absent. Defaults NULL (current working directory). transparent Logical.  Use transparent background.  Defaults FALSE.  warning issued white used fallback JPEG, support transparency. overwrite Logical.  Overwrite existing files TRUE. FALSE (default) Windows-style counter suffix appended, e.g. "plot (1).png". fd_date_stamp Logical.  Appends "MmmDDYYYY" date folder.  Default FALSE. fd_time_stamp Logical.  Appends "HHMM" time folder.  Default FALSE. fn_date_stamp Logical.  Appends date file name.  Default FALSE. fn_time_stamp Logical.  Appends time file name.  Default FALSE.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Called side effect.  Returns full normalised file path(s) saved plot(s) invisibly character vector.

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: Single vs. multi-plot behaviour.  single plot object uses filename exactly (sanitisation).  list multiple elements ignores filename; element's list name becomes file name.  Illegal characters stripped, duplicate names resolved (1), (2), etc. Unnamed list elements.  Elements without name receive placeholder name "plot_N" N position. pondeR result objects.  Objects whose class starts "plot_" matches known pondeR class unpacked automatically. first extractable plot exported; note printed multiple plots present. plotly HTML export.  Requires htmlwidgets.  Static raster export plotly objects requires plotly working reticulate/kaleido installation: reticulate::py_install("kaleido").

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
text: John Lennon L. Calorio

title: Save Plots to Image or Vector Files — save_plots
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  # Single ggplot — filename is respected p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point() save_plots(p, filename = "AUROC All (Neg)")  # List of ggplots — filename is ignored; list names are used p2 <- ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +         geom_point() save_plots(list(scatter = p, iris_plot = p2))  # Accessing a list slot with multiple plots (auto-unpacked) res <- run_auc(data = mydata, response = "group", predictors = c("A","B")) save_plots(res$plots, folder = "figures")  # Override format for all plots save_plots(list(scatter = p, iris_plot = p2), format = "pdf")  # Base-R plot via a zero-argument function save_plots(list(hist_mpg = function() hist(mtcars$mpg, main = "MPG"))) } # }
